source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The <image> shows the high-frequency capacitance-voltage (C-V) characteristics of a Metal/\(SiO_2\)/silicon (MOS) capacitor having an area of \(1 \times 10^{-4} cm^2\). Assume that the permittivities (\(\epsilon_0 \epsilon_r\)) of silicon and SiO2 are \(1 \times 10^{-12}\) F/cm and \(3.5 \times 10^{-13}\) F/cm, respectively.

The maximum depletion layer width in silicon is
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL.Image placeholder rather than a path or URL

issues: metadata.extraction_notes indicates the source image field contained a PIL.Image placeholder rather than a path or URL

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'The figure shows the high-frequency capacitance-voltage (C-V) characteristics...'.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual is central rather than supplemental.
The needed quantity depends on reading C-V information from the plotted figure, which is not fully specified in text alone.
asset_registry_record shows image_manifest exists with 1 PNG image.
multi_step: To find maximum depletion width, the solver must extract one or more capacitance values from the C-V figure and combine them with the given capacitor area and permittivities.
This requires linking visual data from the graph with MOS-capacitor relationships rather than performing a single direct lookup.
The problem is numerical and domain-specific, suggesting at least a small chain of calculations after interpreting the plot.
Multiple-choice options are close in value, so precise interpretation/calculation is likely required.
verifiability: answer_manifest reports answer_type='number', and the provided answer is a specific numeric value '(0.857 μm)'.
A numeric engineering result can be checked by recomputing from the graph-derived capacitance and stated constants.
choices are provided, enabling outcome-level verification against discrete options.
registry_passed=true, so the core assets needed for checking are registered as present.
